C003.claim: The inner product `⟨φ|ψ⟩` encodes the probability amplitude for transitioning from state `|ψ⟩` to outcome `|φ⟩`, which squares to the probability of that outcome.

C003.result_snippet: Bra–ket notation or Dirac notation is a notation for linear algebra and linear operators on complex vector spaces together with their dual spaces both in the finite- and infinite-dimensional cases. Dirac notation, also known as bra-ket notation, is a mathematical notation used in quantum mechanics to describe quantum states and the inner products between them. Dirac Notation, or bra-ket notation, replaces dense matrix arithmetic with a symbolic shorthand that encapsulates vectors, dual vectors, inner products and operators. A state is denoted by a ket vector.The physical significance of the inner product is that it describes the probability amplitude for the particle initially in the state. Bra and ket notations are part of Dirac notation used in quantum mechanics to describe quantum states. A “ket” represents a column vector, while a “bra” represents a row vector, facilitating inner product calculations.
